changelog:
🔒 SECURITY FIX: Removing PIN protection now requires the current PIN. The 'Remove PIN Protection' button in Settings used to work without any verification — anyone with the app open (e.g. a borrowed device left signed in) could disable the lock. The button now asks for the current 4-digit PIN and re-verifies it server-side. Wrong PIN shakes red and shows the error from the server.
🔑 Forgot PIN? link: Added below the PIN-protected actions in AppLock settings. Tapping it opens echowithin.xyz/profile/<you>/settings on the web, where the existing email-based reset flow (6-digit code → new PIN) lives — same flow the website uses, no need to re-implement it in the app.
✅ Mark-all-read no longer 400s: The Activity tab's 'Mark all as read' button was hitting the legacy /api/posts/mark-all-read endpoint, which is CSRF-protected (the Android client doesn't send a CSRF token, so the request got rejected with HTTP 400 and was silently swallowed). Switched the client to the v1 /api/v1/posts/mark-all-read and /api/v1/activity/mark_read routes, which are fully CSRF-exempt. Also moved the matching GETs (badge-counts, my-commented) to v1 so the unread badge, the notifications feed, and the mark-all-read button all read/write the same data model.
🔁 Sync preference now actually respected: In 'Manual Sync' mode, the app used to auto-push pending changes every time connectivity flipped to online (it ignored the syncMode preference on the reconnect path). Fixed: onConnectivityChanged() now early-exits if syncMode != 'automatic', and the LaunchedEffect that consumes the trigger has the same gate as defense in depth. Manual-mode users no longer see their work pushed without their consent.
